lattice.rule: Assurance(module) <= min( statusCap(a) for a in transitive assumptions, maturity(d) for d in transitive annotated deps ). A module whose declared MATURITY exceeds that bound is a build failure.

A_SHA256_COLLISION_RESISTANCE.rationale: Standard cryptographic hardness assumption for SHA-256, relied on by all Merkle constructions.

A_WITNESS_KEY_MANIFEST_AUTHENTIC.rationale: The witness key manifest (trust root) is delivered out-of-band and assumed authentic; no live attestation of its provenance yet.

A_WITNESS_QUORUM_HONEST.rationale: Single-witness (F1) and federation-level disjoint-signer (F2) same-size split views are detected and rejected offline; different-size history rewrites touching a decision's own heads fail via the required consistency proof (F3). Residual: forks among checkpoints outside the supplied set need external gossip monitoring. See docs/research/WITNESS_FORK_TAXONOMY.md.

A_APPEND_ONLY_LOG.rationale: Append-only order is checked at decision time via consistency proofs; the global property depends on a live witness federation that does not exist yet.

A_CALLER_SUPPLIED_ORDERING_UNAUTHENTICAT.rationale: The legacy ledgerAnchoredRevocation detector orders from a caller-supplied sequence with no signatures; by construction this assumption cannot hold.
